claim_gate.reason: The stable frame can now be inverted in polynomial time on two reduction-relevant involution families, but no theorem shows that the resulting measurement outcome identifies the hidden involution or resists classical character/tensor contraction.

falsifiers_triggered: The finite n=8 conditioning pattern was not a numerical accident: exact character-ratio coercivity proves all-n conditioning.
No growing Racah matrix is needed for the conditioning proof or inverse filter.
Conditioning and QSVT implementability alone do not imply useful hidden-involution information.
The theorem covers one conditioned stable branch and two involution families, not the full coset-state ensemble.

operator_theorem.pair_spectrum: spectrum(A_ij)={r_eta: eta in the nine stable intermediate shapes}

positivity_method.certificate: Every shifted numerator and denominator has nonnegative rational coefficients and a positive constant. If denominator degree is d and coefficient sum is D, then B_eta>=c/(D*(u+1)^d)>=c/(D*n^d).

qsvt_filter_contract.complexity: poly(n,log(1/epsilon)) controlled block-encoding uses and gates
qsvt_filter_contract.family_selector: known n and involution class parameter t
qsvt_filter_contract.input: existing polynomial block encoding of F on the stable branch
qsvt_filter_contract.transform: uniform polynomial approximation to x^(-1/2) on the certified positive interval

summary: Proved 54 exact residue/shape coercivity bounds, giving lambda_min(F)>=71/825/n^5 and polynomial inverse-square-root filters for partial and dense matching classes; hidden-involution information and decoding remain open.
